gold_question: What playable races have darkvision out to 60ft?
gold_answer: Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Half-Elf, Half-Orc, Tiefling

gold_question: I am building a rogue character and I want to pick a race that improves my dexterity score. What should I pick?
gold_answer:
Elf and Halfling incease Dexterity by 2. 

Human increases all ability scores by 1 (so includes Dexterity, would be more well rounded). Half-Elf can increase two ability scores by 1, so can choose Dexterity.

gold_question: My player wants to be a cool dragon that spits fire. What race should I recommend?
gold_answer: A Brass, Gold, or Red Draconic Ancestry Dragonborn. Those ancestries have a fire breath weapon.

gold_question: I really want to attack and hit things with my character. Which classes gain an extra attack so I have more chances to hit?
gold_answer: Barbarian, Ranger, Fighter, Monk, Paladin

gold_question: As a barbarian, I get a feature called a Brutal Critical at 9th level. What does it mean that I add an additional weapon damage die? What die do I add?
gold_answer: The die depends on the weapon you are using. So if you are using a Greataxe you would add an additional d12 when rolling damage.

gold_question: What bardic inspiration die should I use at level 7?
gold_answer: At level 7, a bardic inspiration dice would be a d8

gold_question: What rolls can Cutting Words be used on?
gold_answer: Attacks rolls, ability checks, damage rolls

gold_question: My cleric failed to roll for Divine Intervention and my DM said that I can't roll again for 7 days. Is this true?
gold_answer: No, if your character's deity does not intervene, you can roll again once you finish a short rest. If you succeed and your deity intervenes, then you cannot roll again for 7 days.

gold_question: I see that Life Domain clerics have specific Life Domain Spells. What are they? Can't I just pick whatever cleric spells I want?
gold_answer: Domain spells are spells that clerics always have prepared and do not count against the number of spells prepared each day. You can basically think of them as additional prepared spells.

gold_question: At what level can Druids Wildshape into a creature with a flying speed?
gold_answer: Durids can wildshape into a creature with a flying speed at 8th level

gold_question: What class feature allows a character to land a critical hit on a 19 or 20 on the die?
gold_answer:
Fighter with Champion subclass - class feature is Improved Critical

Later feature, Superior Critical allows a critical hit on an 18 - 20

gold_question: If a monk is level 10 and has a +5 to the Dexterity, how would you calculate their Deflect Missiles ability?
gold_answer: The damage would be reduced by 1d10 + 15 (Dexterity Modifier + level)

gold_question: My paladin has a 17 in Constitution, what are their hit points at first level?
gold_answer: 13 : 10 + 3 (Constitution modifier - a 17 in Constitution is equal to a +3 bonus)

gold_question: At level 16, what is a paladin's Lay on Hands Pool?
gold_answer: 80 (5 times paladin level)

gold_question: I know paladins get an aura of some kind. What do they get and at what levels?
gold_answer:
Base class gets Aura of Protection at 6th level and Aura of Courage at 10th level
Oath of Devotion subclass - Aura of Devotion at 7th level

Holy Nimbus could count as an aura - emanate an aura of sunlight at level 20

gold_question: What is favored terrain? What are the options for it? What are the benefits, if any?
gold_answer:
Favored Terrain is a part of the Ranger Class feature Natural Explorer. Options for favored terrain include arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp.

Benefits:
Intelligence or Wisdom Check related to terrain - proficiency bonus is doubled if you are proficient in skill

While travelling in favored terrain for an hour of more:
Difficult terrain doesn't slow group
Group doesn't get lost, except by magic
Remain alert to danger
Can move stealthy at normal pace alone
Find twice as much food when foraging
Learn extra information when tracking creatures

gold_question: How many skills should a rogue have expertise in at 6th level?
gold_answer: 4 skills with expertise - 2 gained at 1st level and 2 more at 6th level

gold_question: At level 13, how much extra damage does a rogue do with their sneak attack?
What is a sorcerer's spellcasting ability?
gold_answer: Charisma

gold_question: May warlock is a Pact of the Chain and is 6th level. Can I add Life Drinker as one of my Eldritch Invocations?
gold_answer:
No - Lifedrinker has 2 prerequisites in order to be selected as an Eldritch Invocations.
1. Need to have Pact of the Blade feature
2. Need to be at least 12th level in order to take this Invocation

gold_question: If I play a 7th level wizard and I was to use Arcane Recovery on a short rest, how many spell slots can I recover?
gold_answer: The spells slots that you can recover can have a combined level of 4 (half wizard level rounded up).

gold_question: A school of evocation wizard finds a scroll of Arcane Hand that they want to transcribe into their spellbook. How long will it take and how much will it cost?
gold_answer:
Arcane Hand is a 5th level evocation spell, so it would take 5 hours and 125gp to transcribe. 

Usually takes 2hours and 50gp per spell level to transcribe a spell, but since they are an Evocation wizard - the time and gold is halved.

gold_question: What classes start with martial weapon proficiency?
gold_answer: Barbarian, Fighter, Paladin, Ranger

gold_question: Can a character still wield a weapon if they are not proficient with it?
gold_answer: Yes. If a character is not proficient with a weapon they can still wield it, but they cannot add their proficiency bonus to the attack roll

gold_question: My ranger wields a longbow and is attacking a creature that is 200 ft away. Does this impact my attack roll?
gold_answer: Yes - the standard range for a longbow is 150 ft. The extended range is 600 ft - this means that you can still attack a creature thatis within that 600 ft range, you just do so with disadvantage.

gold_question: Please list all weapons that have the versatile property
gold_answer: Quarterstaff, Spear, Battleaxe, Longsword, Warhammer, Trident

gold_question: What is the finesse property?
gold_answer: Weapons that have the finesse property can use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls. The same modifer must be used for both rolls.

gold_question: My paladin wields a longsword with two hands and their Strength modifier is +4, what damage do they roll on a hit?
gold_answer: 1d10 + 4 (Longsword is has versatile property, so damage die with two hands is higher - and they also add their strength score to the damage)

gold_question: How much would a basic greataxe cost?
gold_answer: 30 gold pieces

gold_question: A character has a -1 Wisdom modifier. What do they need to roll to use a Healer's Kit to stabilize a creature with 0 hit points?
gold_answer: They do not need to roll a check to use a Healer's Kit. It has 10 uses, a character can expend a single use as an action to stabilize a creature without making a medicine (wisdom) check

gold_question: If a wizard casts Hold Person (concentration) and then on their next turn uses Action Surge from a multiclass fighter level to cast another concentration spell, which spell remains active?
gold_answer: The second spell remains active and Hold Person drops

gold_question: Can a sorcerer use Subtle Spell metamagic to cast a spell while under the effect of Silence?
gold_answer: Yes they can, Subtle Spell allows a spell to be cast without any somatic or verbal components

gold_question: If a character is concentrating on a spell and casts a spell with a casting time of 1 minute, at what point do they lose concentration on the first spell?
gold_answer: They would lose concentration the second they start casting the spell that takes 1 minute, as they need to concentrate on the spell with the longer casting time

gold_question: Does the Shield spell, cast as a reaction, interrupt concentration on another spell being cast in the same round?
gold_answer: No, no concentration is required for Shield. Therefore, it would not interrupt a concentration spell.

gold_question: If a Cleric casts Spiritual Weapon (bonus action) and has already cast a leveled spell using their action this turn via some means, is this legal under spellcasting rules?
gold_answer: No, this is not legal under spellcasting rules. You can only cast a level spell and a cantrip on your turn.

gold_question: Can you cast a spell with a range of Touch on yourself if you are restrained but not incapacitated?
gold_answer: Yes, if a you are restrained but not incapacitated you can cast a spell with a range of touch on yourself

gold_question: If a spell requires verbal components and the caster is underwater without magical means to breathe/speak, can the spell be cast?
gold_answer: No, they cannot cast the spell because they cannot make the specific sounds required under water

gold_question: When a spell says it lasts "until dispelled," does that spell still count against the caster's prepared spells known/slots?
gold_answer: It counts against there known spells and spell slots if they wish to prepare it, and during the initial casting. However, once the spell is cast the maintaing of it does not count against their known spells or slots - the spell just lasts

gold_question: If a concentration spell has a duration of "up to 1 minute" and the caster stops concentrating after 30 seconds, do they get the spell slot back?
gold_answer: No, once the spell is cast, the spell slot is gone, even if they drop concentration early

gold_question: Can a character ready a spell as an action, use their concentration to maintain a different spell, and when the readied spell triggers, maintain concentration on the new spell instead?
gold_answer: No, readying a spell as an action takes concentration, so once they decide to ready a spell, their concentration would drop on the spell they are currently maintaining. They cannot hold concentration on one spell while waiting for the other to trigger.

gold_question: If a creature is grappled and then uses the Disengage action, can they move without provoking opportunity attacks from creatures other than their grappler?
gold_answer: When a creature is grappled, their speed is reduced to 0, so even if they use the disengage action, they cannot move away at all

gold_question: If a creature uses their movement to move 15 feet, gets knocked prone, crawls 10 feet (using 20 feet of movement), stands up (using 15 feet), do they have enough movement to walk 5 more feet if their speed is 30?
gold_answer: If their movement speed is 30 feet, they wouldn't have enough movement to even stand. If they use their action to Dash, that would give them 60 feet of movement then they would have enough to move that additional 5 feet as that would bring them to 50 feet of movement.

gold_question: Can a creature with 0 movement remaining take the Dash action to gain movement?
gold_answer: If they take the Dash action, they would regain movement equal to their movement speed - ie if they have 30 feet of movement they would be able to move 30 feet with the Dash action

gold_question: If a flying creature is knocked prone mid-flight, do they fall immediately or at the end of their turn?
gold_answer: The creature would begin falling they minute their knocked prone unless they have the ability of hover or be held aloft by magic

gold_question: If a creature is subjected to forced movement (e.g., Thunderwave) and pushed through multiple creatures' reach, how many opportunity attacks can be made?
gold_answer: No opportunity attacks can be made against a creature that is subjected to forced movement

gold_question: Can a creature with a reach weapon make opportunity attacks against enemies 10 feet away if those enemies move from 10 feet to 5 feet away?
gold_answer: No, that is the creature moving into your space, rather than out of your reach which is what triggers the opportunity attack

gold_question: Does a critical hit on an attack that also applies a saving throw effect (like a poisoned weapon) affect the saving throw DC or the poison damage?
gold_answer: It does not impact the saving throw DC, that remains the same despite the critical. The damage for the saving throw would not be included in the doubled damage as it is a separate saving throw to the initial to hit.

gold_question: If a paladin uses Divine Smite on a critical hit, are the smite dice also doubled?
gold_answer: yes, the divine smite damage is double as it is part of the initial hit

gold_question: Can an unarmed strike be made as part of the Attack action if the character is wielding a two-handed weapon?
gold_answer: Yes, the weapon only requires two hands when making an attack with it

gold_question: When an attack roll is made with a ranged weapon against a prone target, does the attack have advantage if you're within 5 feet and disadvantage because they're prone?
gold_answer: If a character has advantage and disadvantage, they cancel out and it is a straight attack roll

gold_question: If a Rogue has two chances to apply Sneak Attack in a round (reaction opportunity attack and regular attack), can they apply it twice?
gold_answer: Yes, a rogue can apply their sneak attack as a reaction and as a regualr attack

gold_question: If a creature is both paralyzed and petrified, which condition's rules take precedence for being attacked?
gold_answer: Both rules apply at the same time, neither take precedence both paralyzed and petrified conditions occur simultaneously

gold_question: Can a blinded creature make opportunity attacks against creatures it can hear?
gold_answer: No, the rule as written is that when a creature you can see moves out of range. A blinded creature cannot see someone move out of range, even if it can hear

gold_question: If a creature is invisible and also has total cover, do attackers have disadvantage on attack rolls against it, or can they not target it at all?
gold_answer: If a creature has total cover and is invisible it cannot be targetted directly by a spell or attack. Some AOE spells can still effect the creature

gold_question: Does the frightened condition prevent a creature from making opportunity attacks against the source of their fear?
gold_answer: No, they would have disadvantage on the attack roll if the source of their fear is in their line of sight, but they can still make attack rolls

gold_question: If a creature is charmed by one caster and another caster attempts to charm the same creature, what happens?
gold_answer: If it is the same spell, the one with the higher DC would take precedence. If they are 2 different kinds of charm effects, then they would stack

gold_question: Can a creature that is incapacitated still maintain concentration on a spell if they don't take damage?
gold_answer: No, creatures lose concentration on a spell if they are incapacitated

gold_question: If a creature is polymorphed into a form that doesn't have the capability to maintain concentration (like a fish), do they lose concentration on spells?
gold_answer: A creature should not lose concentration as it does not fall into any of the reasons as to why they would lose concentration on spells. They would only lose concentration on a previous spell if they are the one casting polymorph

gold_question: Does being unconscious automatically end concentration, or only if you're reduced to 0 hit points?
gold_answer: Yes, unconscious automatically drops concentration

gold_question: If a creature has advantage on Dexterity saving throws and the Evasion feature, and they roll a natural 1 on one die but succeed on the other, what happens?
gold_answer: Then they succeed on the roll due to having advantage, and becuase they have evasion, they take no damage from the effect or attack

gold_question: Can you use the Help action to give advantage on a skill check that will be made outside of combat?
gold_answer: Yes, the help action can be used outside of combat to help give another player advantage on an ability check

gold_question: If a creature is subjected to two effects that each require the same saving throw on the same turn, do they make one save or two?
gold_answer: They need to make 2 saving throws as they are still 2 separate effects even if they are the same saving throw skill

gold_question: Does the Bless spell's 1d4 bonus apply to death saving throws?
gold_answer: Yes, death saving throws are special saving throws, but are saving throws none the less

gold_question: If a creature has proficiency in a saving throw from class features and proficiency from another source, do they add their proficiency bonus twice?
gold_answer: No, creatures can only add their proficiency bonus twice if they have a way to gain expertise in a skill

gold_question: If an effect forces a creature to make a saving throw but they're incapacitated, do they automatically fail only Strength and Dexterity saves?
gold_answer: Incapacitated does not state that a creature automatically fails any saving throws, it just cannot take actions or reactions, so saving throws should be rolled as normal, unless another condition is also in place

gold_question: Does a Paladin's Aura of Protection add to their own saving throws if they're making a save?
gold_answer: Yes, whenever you or a friendly creature within 10 feet makes a saving throw they get to add the aura bonus

gold_question: Can a Monk use Flurry of Blows if they took the Attack action but didn't make any unarmed strikes with it?
gold_answer: Yes, they only need to take the attack action to use flurry of blows, they do not need to specifically make an unarmed strike

gold_question: If a Barbarian/Druid rages and then wild shapes, do they retain the rage benefits?
gold_answer: You should still retain your rage as Wild Shape still allows you to retain any features from your class

gold_question: Does a Rogue's Sneak Attack work with spell attacks like Scorching Ray?
gold_answer: No, sneak attack only works with a finesse or ranged weapon

gold_question: If a multiclassed spellcaster (Cleric 3/Wizard 2) casts a spell using a Wizard slot, does it count as a Wizard spell or a Cleric spell for purposes of features that distinguish between them?
gold_answer: The spell slots do not determine the spell's narture. Multiclass spellcasters have on pool of spell slots, the type of spell is determined by the class spell list and if it prepared as part of their cleric ritual or written in their wizard spell book that will determine what spell casting modifier they can use and any additional class features or benefits that they receive

gold_question: Can a Warlock use their Pact Magic slots to cast spells they know from multiclassing into another full caster class?
gold_answer: Yes, Warlock's can use their pact spells to cast spells they have received from multiclassing

gold_question: If a Sorcerer uses the Careful Spell metamagic on an AoE spell and allies in the area succeed on their saving throws, do they take half damage or no damage?
gold_answer: They would take half damage as they succeed on the spell save, and are protected from the full force of the spell, but are not fully protected from the spell

gold_question: If a character takes a short rest but is interrupted with 1 minute remaining, do they gain the benefits of the short rest?
gold_answer: Technically, if they are interrupted even a minute before they complete the full one hour short rest, they do not gain the benefits of a short rest at all

gold_question: Can a character attune to a magic item during a short rest if they're also using Hit Dice to recover hit points?
gold_answer: Yes, a creature can recuperate and regain hit points while focusing on attuning to a magic item

gold_question: If a Wizard uses Arcane Recovery during a short rest, can they then cast ritual spells without expending spell slots during the remainder of that short rest?
gold_answer: No, a wizard cannot use their Arcane Recovery if they cast a ritual spell as a ritual spell it is too strenuous an activity to be considered a short rest.

gold_question: During a long rest, if a character is woken up to stand watch for 2 hours and then goes back to sleep, is the long rest interrupted?
gold_answer: No, the long rest is not interrupted as a 2 hour watch is not considered strenuous activity so it does not interrupt the rest

gold_question: Can a character benefit from more than one long rest in a 24-hour period if they have time?
gold_answer: No, a character can only benefit from one long rest is a 24 hour period.

gold_question: If a creature is wearing adamantine armor (which turns critical hits into normal hits) and is also paralyzed (which causes attack hits within 5 feet to be criticals), what happens?
gold_answer: The wording of adamantine armor states that it turns critical hits into normal hits, so even if the character is paralyxed they would still gain the benefits of the armor converting the critical hits to normal ones

gold_question: Can a character don or doff a shield as part of drawing or stowing another weapon?
gold_answer: No, it takes an action to don or doff a shield.

gold_question: Does a +1 weapon overcome resistance to non-magical damage?
gold_answer: Yes, a +1 weapon is magical for the purpose of overcoming resistances

gold_question: If a magical weapon has both a +1 bonus and an additional property, does it count as one or two attuned items?
gold_answer: The weapon only counts as attuned if it is an attuned weapon. It is not automatically an atuned weapon just because it has specific magical properties. Also attunement is based on the item, not the number of magical abilities the item has

gold_question: If a creature uses the Ready action to ready a spell, moves, and then the trigger occurs, do they cast the spell from their original position or their new position?
gold_answer: If they move, they cast the spell from their new position. If they can choose where the spell goes off, it goes off in that location as long as the character is still in range of that location

gold_question: Can a creature grapple another creature that is two sizes larger than them if they have advantage on the check?
gold_answer: No, even with advantage, a creature can only grapple another creature that is up to one size larger than them

gold_question: If multiple spells are in effect that change a creature's speed (like Longstrider and Slow), how do you determine their final speed?
gold_answer: Both spell effects would be active simultaneously, unless there is some callout that magic cannot effect a character's speed. So in the case of Longstrider and Slow, they would have the additional speed from Longstrider, but if they fail their saving throw for slow, then that max speed from Longstrider would be halved.

gold_question: If a creature polymorphed into a form with legendary resistance uses that resistance, and then the polymorph ends, do they still have access to legendary resistance?
gold_answer: No, once the polymorph ends, they no longer have any of the resistances or abilities that were unique to the creature that they polymorphed into

gold_question: Can you use a spell like Dimension Door to teleport while grappling a creature if you bring them with you?
gold_answer: Dimension Door allows you to bring one willing creature, your size or smaller. So if the creature you are grappling is willing and your size or smaller, you can bring them. If they are unwilling, or larger than you, you cannot - this would end the grapple as you teleport away

gold_question: If a Druid wild shapes into a creature with a climb speed and is climbing a wall when the wild shape ends, what happens?
gold_answer: If the druid originally has a climb speed, they could potentially continue climbing. If not, they may be required to make a Strength (Athletics) to hold on or continue the climb, and the climb would cost double movement (ie 1 foot = 2 feet)

gold_question: If a character has levels in both the Barbarian and Monk classes, can they benefit from both the Barbarian's Unarmored Defense (10 + Dex + Con) and the Monk's Unarmored Defense (10 + Dex + Wis) simultaneously?
gold_answer: No, they would only benefit from one of the armored defenses - which ever class you started with. It would not stack.

gold_question: A 5th-level Barbarian / 5th-level Fighter has the Extra Attack feature from both classes. How many total attacks can they make when they take the Attack action?
gold_answer: They would only be able to attack twice, they do not get the extra attack benefit from both classes

gold_question: If a Rogue/Paladin multiclass makes a melee weapon attack with a finesse weapon while they have disadvantage, but an ally is within 5 feet of the target, can they use Divine Smite and Sneak Attack on the same hit?
gold_answer: A rogue cannot use their sneak attack if they have disadvantage, even if an ally is within 5 feet. So they would not be able to add their sneak attack to the hit, but they could add their Divine Smite.

gold_question: When a Druid is in Wild Shape and transformed into a beast, can they use the Monk’s Martial Arts feature to make an unarmed strike as a bonus action?
gold_answer: Yes, when a druid is wild shape they can use any class features that are available to them (outside of casting spells, or outside of the capability of the creature) as long as the conditions for the feature can be met

gold_question: Does a Bard’s Jack of All Trades feature apply to their Initiative rolls?
gold_answer: Yes, Bards gain a bonus to the their initiative roll as part of the Jack of All Trades Feature

gold_question: If a multiclass character has 3 levels in Wizard and 4 levels in Sorcerer, what is the maximum level of Wizard spell they can have in their spellbook, and what is the highest level spell slot they possess?
gold_answer: The maximum level wizard spell they can have in their spellbook is level 2, and the highest spell slot should be 4th level spell slot

gold_question: If a Rogue uses the Uncanny Dodge reaction against an attack, but is also wearing armor they are not proficient in, how does the disadvantage from the armor interact with the damage reduction?
gold_answer: The armor disadvantage should not affect the damage reduction

gold_question: A 20th-level Druid (Archdruid) is in Wild Shape. Can they cast a spell that requires a verbal component while in a zone of Silence?
gold_answer: Yes, they can still cast when in the zone of a Silence spell because they ignore verbal and somatic components of their Druid spells in both their normal and Wild Shape form

gold_question: If a Barbarian is Raging and has the Mindless Rage feature (Path of the Berserker), what happens to the Frightened condition if it was already affecting them before they entered their Rage?
gold_answer: If they enter Mindless Rage with the Frightened condition already affecting them, then the condition is suspended (not active) while they are raging. The condition returns if their rage ends, unless it has be ended in some other way while raging

gold_question: Can a Fighter with the Great Weapon Fighting style reroll a 1 or 2 on the extra damage dice granted by a Paladin’s Divine Smite?
gold_answer: No, the divine smite is additional spell damage added to the roll. Great Weapon Fighting only allows a reroll on the damage made with a melee weapon that is wielded with 2 hands

gold_question: A character with the Extra Attack feature is wielding a Heavy Crossbow. How many times can they attack with that weapon using their Action?
gold_answer: Because a heavy crossbow has the loading property, you can only fire one shot when taking the Attack action, even if you have 2 attacks

gold_question: If a character is Grappled by a creature, can they still use their Reaction to make an Opportunity Attack against a different creature moving out of their reach?
gold_answer: Yes, they can still use their reaction to make an Opportunity Attack. Grappled just reduces their speed to 0

gold_question: Does the Shield spell’s +5 bonus to AC apply to the specific attack roll that triggered the reaction to cast it?
gold_answer: Yes it applies to the triggering attack, and then the AC Bonus lasts until the start of their next turn

gold_question: If an attacker is Invisible and makes a melee attack against a target that is Prone, does the attacker have Advantage, Disadvantage, or a normal roll?
gold_answer: The attacker has advantage because the creature is prone and because they are invisible, but the advantage does not stack

gold_question: A character is Restrained and Prone. An enemy attacks them from 10 feet away with a reach weapon. What is the state of the attacker’s Advantage or Disadvantage?
gold_answer: The attacker has advantage from the restrained condition, but disadvantage because the creature is prone and they are more than 5 feet away. This means that they have a normal/straight roll

gold_question: Can a character use the Help action to give an ally advantage on an attack roll if the character themselves is currently Blinded?
gold_answer: Yes, if there is a creature within 5 feet of them. They can feint and make themselves look more vulnerable to an attack, allowing their ally to gain advantage. Blinded should not prevent this

gold_question: When a character uses the Ready action to cast a spell, and the trigger does not occur before the start of their next turn, what happens to the spell slot used?
gold_answer: The spell slot is expended, even if the triggering event does not occur

gold_question: If a creature is Squeezing into a smaller space and attempts to make an attack, how does its state affect the attack roll?
gold_answer: If they are squeezing into a smaller space, they have disadvantage on any attack rolls, while in the small space

gold_question: Does a creature’s Passive Perception score change if they are currently traveling at a "Fast" pace?
gold_answer: If creatures are moving at a "Fast" pace they have a -5 penalty to their Passive Perception

gold_question: If a character is Stabilized at 0 hit points but then takes 1 point of damage from a non-lethal source, does their status change?
gold_answer: Yes, if a creature that is stable takes any damage, they must start making death saving throws again

gold_question: Can a character use the Dash action while they are Heavily Encumbered under the variant encumbrance rules?
gold_answer: yes, they can still use the dash action their speed would just be reduced by 20 feet

gold_question: If an attacker cannot see their target, but knows their location, and the target is behind Half Cover, does the attacker roll with Disadvantage, or does the cover bonus simply increase the target's AC?
gold_answer:
This depends on the type of attack - if it requires line of sight on the target, even if the attacker knows the location, they still will be unable to attack the creature. 

If they do not need line of site, then they can attack and half-cover will add a +2 bonuse to the target AC and Dexterity Saving throws. If they need to make a saving throw against the attack, there is no bonus unless it is a Dexterity Saving throw. If there is an attack roll, they may roll with disadvantage - such as firing an arrow

gold_question: Does a character who has been "knocked unconscious" by a melee attack that they chose to make non-lethal still have to roll death saving throws?
gold_answer: No, if the attacks are non-lethal, then they do not need to make death saving throws unless they are reduced to 0 and take any additional damage.

gold_question: When using the Two-Weapon Fighting rule, can a character use a bonus action to make an attack if their first attack was made with a Thrown weapon that is no longer in their hand?
gold_answer: Yes, they can still make the bonus action attack even if they have thrown their first weapon as long as both weapons have the light property

gold_question: If a character is Mounted and their mount is forced to move by a Thunderwave spell, what specific saving throw must the rider make to avoid falling off?
gold_answer: They must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity Saving Throw to avoid falling off the mount

gold_question: A Sorcerer uses the Twinned Spell Metamagic on a spell that normally targets one creature. If the spell is cast using a higher-level slot that allows it to target multiple creatures, is it still eligible for Twinned Spell?
gold_answer: No, they can only use twinned spell on a spell that targets only one creature

gold_question: If a spell has a casting time of "1 minute," and the caster takes damage on their third turn of casting, must they make a Constitution saving throw to maintain the casting?
gold_answer: Yes, they must make a constitution saving throw to continue concentrating on the spell. If they lose concentration, they do no lose the spell slot, but they will need to start the casting all over again

gold_question: Can a Wizard cast a spell as a Ritual if that spell is in their spellbook but is not one of their currently prepared spells?
gold_answer: Yes, wizards can cast ritual spells (only as a ritual) even if the spell in not one of their prepared spells for the day

gold_question: If a creature has Resistance to all damage and is hit by an attack that deals 11 damage, what is the final amount of damage recorded?
gold_answer: The final damage would be 5 damage as you would round down for uneven damage

gold_question: Does the Invisibility spell end if the invisible creature uses its action to force another creature to make a saving throw (such as via a Class Feature) without actually "casting a spell"?
gold_answer: No, the spell just ends if they attack or cast a spell, as long as the class feature is not one of these, then they would not lose their invisibility

gold_question: If a character has 10 Temporary Hit Points and takes 15 damage from a single source, and then receives 10 more Temporary Hit Points from a different source on the next turn, what is their current Temporary Hit Point total?
gold_answer: Their current temporary hit points is 10. Temporary hit points do not stack, and cannot be healed once lost. So they would still have damage, and then would have 10 temporary hit points on top of their lowered actual hit points

gold_question: If a spell has a duration of "Concentration, up to 1 minute," and the caster is knocked Unconscious, when exactly does the spell end?
gold_answer: The spell ends as soon as they are knocked unconscious because they can no longer concentrate on the spell

gold_question: A Warlock with the Devil’s Sight invocation is standing in the area of a Darkness spell. Does a creature outside the darkness have Advantage or Disadvantage when attacking the Warlock with a ranged weapon?
gold_answer: The person attacking from outside the darkness would have disadvantage as they cannot see the warlock inside the darkness spell so do not know where to attack

gold_question: Can a Sorcerer use the Subtle Spell Metamagic to cast a spell while they are Grappled and Restrained?
gold_answer: A sorcerer can use subtle spell to cast as spell while grappled and restrained. They do not have to use it, as the conditions do not effect their spellcasting, but they can if they want

gold_question: If a character is under the effect of two different spells that both provide a bonus to AC (such as Shield of Faith and Haste), do the bonuses stack?
gold_answer: Yes, the are separate spells so they should stack

gold_question: What happens if a character is at 0 hit points, has 5 Temporary Hit Points, and is hit by an attack that deals 3 damage?
gold_answer: Temporary Hit Points do not bring you back up from 0. So the 5 temp hp would absorb the damage, leaving you with 2 temp hp, and you would still need to make a death saving throw for taking damage while at 0 hit points

gold_question: If a spell’s range is Touch, can a Sorcerer use Distant Spell to make the range 30 feet?
gold_answer: Yes, they can make the range 30 feet with distant spell

gold_question: Does the Bless spell allow a character to add 1d4 to their own Concentration saving throw?
gold_answer: Yes, they can add bless as they are making a constitution saving throw to maintain concetration

gold_question: Can a creature be affected by two identical spells (e.g., two Longstrider spells) if they are cast by two different spellcasters?
gold_answer: No, spells of the same type do not stack to improve a character's abilities

gold_question: When casting a spell with a Material component that has a specific gold cost, can a Spellcasting Focus be used in its place?
gold_answer: No, if a material component has a specific gold cost, then that cost is needed to cast the spell even with a spellcasting focus

gold_question: If a character is wearing Heavy Armor but does not meet the Strength requirement, how is their movement speed affected if they are also moving through Difficult Terrain?
gold_answer: If a character does not have the necessary strength to wear heavy armor, their speed is reduced by 10 feet. If areas of difficult terrain, their lowered speed would then be reduced to half while navigating through the difficult terrain

gold_question: Can a Small creature use a weapon with the Heavy property without disadvantage if they have a Strength score of 20?
gold_answer: No, even if their strength is 20, a small creature cannot weild a heavy weapon without disadvantage due to their size

gold_question: How does the Underwater Combat rule affect a ranged weapon attack made with a Longbow against a target within the weapon's normal range?
gold_answer: Any attack from a longbow made underwater within its normal range will have disadvantage on the attack

gold_question: If a character falls into a pit, does the Monk's Slow Fall feature reduce the damage before or after the 1d6 per 10 feet is calculated?
gold_answer: After, slow fall reduces any falling damage by 5 times your monk level

gold_question: Does a character with Darkvision see in "Dim Light" as if it were "Bright Light," or as if it were "Darkness"?
gold_answer: They can see in dim light as it it were bright light

gold_question: If a character is Hidden and moves 10 feet across an open floor to a new piece of cover, at what point (if any) is their Hidden status automatically lost?
gold_answer: Their hidden status is lost as soon as they are no longer behind cover, even with a good stealth roll, once they are in an area where they can be seen clearly, they are no longer hidden

gold_question: Can a character Don a shield using only a Bonus Action if they have the Fast Hands feature (Rogue Thief)?
gold_answer: No because donning a shield takes an action, it is not just an object interaction
